title: Sour Crispy Turkish Padron Peppers Stir-fry
description: This dish features tender yet slightly crunchy padron peppers in a tangy sauce made with lemon juice and vinegar, perfect for adding to your dinner menu.
instructions: Clean the padron peppers and remove their stems, seeds, and membranes.
In a pan over medium heat, add olive oil. Once hot, sauté minced garlic until fragrant.
Add padron peppers to the pan along with salt; stir occasionally for about 2-3 minutes or until they start browning slightly.
Pour in lemon juice and apple cider vinegar; mix well and let it cook for another minute until most of the liquid has evaporated.
Sprinkle chili flakes over the mixture, toss gently to coat all peppers evenly. Serve immediately garnished with fresh parsley.
Enjoy your meal hot as a starter or side dish.
